message:
fix(tmux): use Enter key instead of C-m for prompt submission

Some TUIs (notably Codex) distinguish between the Enter key and the
C-m (Ctrl+M) control character. While C-m is the ASCII carriage return
and historically equivalent to Enter in many contexts, modern TUI
applications may treat them differently.

This change ensures prompts are properly submitted to Codex agents
by sending the actual "Enter" key rather than the C-m control sequence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
